hadiths.chapter: 156. CHAPTER ON A MAN BORROWING TO PERFORM
hadiths.en:
Musa ibn Bakr narrated from Imam {a.s}, who said:  
I asked him: "Can a man borrow money to perform Hajj if he has resources left behind 
to settle the debt in case something happens to him?"  
Imam {a.s} said: "Yes."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 2 • Al-Kafi, Vol. 4, p. 279 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 12, p. 269 • Wasa’il 
Al-Shi’a, Vol. 11, p. 142

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 2, Ch. 156, H. 2903
reference.chapter: CHAPTER ON A MAN BORROWING TO PERFORM
reference.location: Volume 2 › CHAPTER ON A MAN BORROWING TO PERFORM

hadiths.chapter: 156. CHAPTER ON A MAN BORROWING TO PERFORM HAJJ AND THE OBLIGATION OF HAJJ ON SOMEONE IN DEBT
hadiths.en:
It was narrated from Abu Hammam, who said:   
I said to Al-Ridha {a.s}: "A man has debt, and some wealth comes to him. Should he pay 
his debt or perform Hajj?"  
Imam {a.s} said: "He should pay part of it toward his debt and use part for Hajj."   
I said: "What if the amount is only sufficient for the expenses of Hajj?"  
Imam {a.s} said: "Let him pay his debt one year and perform Hajj the next year."   
I asked: "What if the money comes from the government?"  
Imam {a.s} said: "There is no harm for you in that."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 2 • Al-Kafi, Vol. 4, p. 279 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 12, p. 270 • Wasa’il 
Al-Shi’a, Vol. 11, p. 141  
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 2, Ch. 156, H. 2904
reference.chapter: CHAPTER ON A MAN BORROWING TO PERFORM HAJJ AND THE OBLIGATION OF HAJJ ON SOMEONE IN DEBT
reference.location: Volume 2 › CHAPTER ON A MAN BORROWING TO PERFORM HAJJ AND THE OBLIGATION OF HAJJ ON SOMEONE IN DEBT

hadiths.chapter: 156. CHAPTER ON A MAN BORROWING TO PERFORM HAJJ AND THE OBLIGATION OF HAJJ ON SOMEONE IN DEBT
hadiths.ar:
2906   -  وَّ رَّوَّى إِن ْن ُّ مَّحْن ُّوب ٍ عَّن ْ إ َّن َّان ٍ عَّن ِ إَّلْحَّسَّن ِ ن ْن ِ ر ِي َّاذٍ إَّلْعَّط َّارِ ق َّالَّ : ق ُّلْث ُّ لِأ َّن ِى  عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّلَّي ْ هِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ ي َّكُّون ُّ 
عَّلَّى  َّ إَّلذ َّي ْن ُّ  ق َّي َّق َّغُّ ق ِى  ي َّذِى َّ إَّلذ َّرَّإهِمُّ ق َّا ِن ْ وَّر  َّعْي ُّهَّا ن َّي ْن َّهُّمْ لَّمْ ي َّق َّغْ س َّي ْن اً إ َّ ق َّا َّجُّح  ُّ إ َّوْ إ ُّوَّر ِّعُّهَّا ن َّي ْن َّ إَّ لْع ُّزَّمَّاءِ ق َّال 
وَّ إُّذْعُّ إَّلل َّهَّ إ َّن ْ ي َّق ْص ِى َّ عَّن ْك َّ ذَّي ْن َّك َّ إ ِن ْ س َّاءَّ إَّلل َّهُّ ي َّعَّالَّى
hadiths.en:
A man asked Abu Abdullah {a.s}: "I am a man in debt. Should I borrow money 
and perform Hajj?"  
Imam {a.s} replied: "Yes, it is more effective in settling the debt."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 2  
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 2, Ch. 156, H. 2905

hadiths.chapter: 156. CHAPTER ON A MAN BORROWING TO PERFORM HAJJ AND THE OBLIGATION OF HAJJ ON SOMEONE IN DEBT
hadiths.en:
Ibn Mahbub narrated from Aban, from Al-Hasan ibn Ziyad al-‘Attar, who said:  
I said to Abu Abdullah {a.s}: "I have debt, and some dirhams come into my possession. 
If I divide them among my creditors, they will not settle anything significant. Should I 
use them for Hajj, or should I distribute them among the creditors?"   
Imam {a.s} said: "Perform Hajj with them, and pray to Allah {SWT} to help you settle your 
debt, God willing."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 2 • Al-Kafi, Vol. 4, p. 279 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 12, p. 271 • Wasa’il 
Al-Shi’a, Vol. 11, p. 142

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 2, Ch. 156, H. 2906

hadiths.chapter: 157. CHAPTER ON WHAT HAS BEEN REPORTED
hadiths.ar: 2908 - وَّ ق ِى  رِوَّإي َّه ِ عَّن ْذِ إَّلز َّجْمَّن ِ ن ْن ِ إ َّن ِى  عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّن ِ إَّلص َّاذِق ِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ ق َّال  رَّع ِمَّ إ َّن ْق ُّه
hadiths.en:
Aban narrated from Zurara, from Abu Ja'far {a.s}, who said:  
I asked Imam {a.s} about a woman who has a husband, has not performed Hajj before 
(sarura), and her husband does not permit her to go for Hajj.  
Imam {a.s} said: "She should perform Hajj, even if he does not permit her."   
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 2 • Al-Kafi, Vol. 4, p. 282 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 12, p. 280 • Wasa’il 
Al-Shi’a, Vol. 11, p. 156  
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 2, Ch. 157, H. 2907
reference.chapter: CHAPTER ON WHAT HAS BEEN REPORTED
reference.location: Volume 2 › CHAPTER ON WHAT HAS BEEN REPORTED
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ١٥٧، ح ٢٩٠٧

hadiths.chapter: 157. CHAPTER ON WHAT HAS BEEN REPORTED
hadiths.ar:
2909  - وَّ رَّوَّى إ ِسْحَّاق ُّ ن ْن ُّ عَّم َّارٍ عَّن ْ إ َّن ِى  إ ِن ْزَّإهِي مَّ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ ق َّالَّ : سَّا َّلْي ُّهُّ عَّن ِ إَّلْمَّزْإ َّة ِ إَّلْمُّوسِزَّة ِ ق َّذْ جَّح  َّث ْ جَّح  َّه َّ 
إَّلْأ ِسْلاَّمِ ق َّي َّق ُّولُّ لِز َّوْج ِهَّا إ َّ جِح  َّن ِى  مَّز َّة ً إ ُّج ْزَّى إ َّ لَّهُّ إ َّن ْ ي َّمْن َّعَّهَّا ق َّال ا جَّق ِّى  عَّلَّي ْك ِ إ َّعْط َّمُّ مِن ْ جَّق ِّك ِ عَّلَّى  َّ  
ق ِى  ذ َّإ
hadiths.en:
In a narration from Abdul -Rahman ibn Abi Abdullah, from Imam Jafar ibn 
Muhammad Al-Sadiq {a.s},  
Imam {a.s} said: "She should perform Hajj, even if it displeases him."   
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 2  
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 2, Ch. 157, H. 2908
reference.chapter: CHAPTER ON WHAT HAS BEEN REPORTED
reference.location: Volume 2 › CHAPTER ON WHAT HAS BEEN REPORTED
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ١٥٧، ح ٢٩٠٨

hadiths.chapter: 157. CHAPTER ON WHAT HAS BEEN REPORTED
hadiths.en:
Ishaq ibn Ammar narrated from Abu Ibrahim {a.s}, who said:  
I asked him about a wealthy woman who had already performed the Hajj of Islam but 
said to her husband, "Take me for Hajj again." Can he prevent her from going?  
Imam {a.s} said: "Yes, he can say to her, 'My right over you is greater than your right over 
me in this matter.'"  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 2

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 2, Ch. 157, H. 2909

hadiths.chapter: 158. CHAPTER ON A WOMAN PERFORMING HAJJ
hadiths.ar:
2911  -  وَّ ق ِى  رِوَّإي َّه ِ هِس َّامٍ عَّن ْ سُّلَّي ْمَّان َّ ن ْن ِ ج َّالِذٍ عَّن ْ إ َّن ِى  عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ : ق ِى  إَّلْمَّزْإ َّة ِ ي ُّزِ ي ذُّ إَّلْحَّح  َّ وَّ لَّي ْسَّ  
مَّعَّهَّا مَّحْزَّم  هَّلْ ي َّصْلُّحُّ لَّهَّا إَّلْحَّح  ُّ ق َّق َّال
hadiths.en:
It was narrated from Mu'awiyah ibn Ammar, who said:   
I asked Abu Abdullah {a.s} about a woman traveling to Mecca without a guardian.  
Imam {a.s} said: "There is no harm if she travels with trustworthy people."   
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 2 • Al-Kafi, Vol. 4, p. 282 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 12, p. 281 • Wasa’il 
Al-Shi’a, Vol. 11, p. 153  
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 2, Ch. 158, H. 2910
reference.chapter: CHAPTER ON A WOMAN PERFORMING HAJJ
reference.location: Volume 2 › CHAPTER ON A WOMAN PERFORMING HAJJ
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ١٥٨، ح ٢٩١٠

hadiths.chapter: 158. CHAPTER ON A WOMAN PERFORMING HAJJ
hadiths.ar:
2912  -  وَّ رَّوَّى إَّلْن َّز َّن ْطِى  ُّ عَّن ْ صَّق ْوَّإن َّ إَّلْح َّم َّالِ ق َّالَّ : ق ُّلْث ُّ لِأ َّن ِى  عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ ق َّذْ عَّزَّق ْي ن ِى  ن ِعَّمَّلِى  ي َّا ْي ِي ن ِى   
إَّلْمَّزْإ َّة ُّ إ َّعْزِق ُّهَّا ن ِا ِسْلاَّمِهَّا وَّ جُّن ِّهَّا إ ِي  َّاكُّمْ وَّ وَّلأَّي َّي ِهَّا لَّكُّمْ لَّي ْسَّ لَّهَّا   مَّحْزَّم  ق َّال 
إَّلْمُّو ْمِن َّ مَّحْزَّمُّ إَّلْمُّو ْمِن َّه ِ ۝ وَّ إَّلْمُّو ْمِن ُّون َّ وَّ إَّلْمُّو ْمِن ٰاب ُّ ن َّعْص ُّهُّمْ إ َّوْلِي ٰاءُّ ن َّعْص ٍ ۝
hadiths.en:
In a narration from Hisham, from Sulayman ibn Khalid, from Abu Abdullah 
{a.s}, regarding a woman who wants to perform Hajj but does not have a mahram (close 
male relative), he {a.s} was asked: "Is it permissible for her to perform Hajj?"  
Imam {a.s} said: "Yes, if she is trustworthy."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 2 • Al-Kafi, Vol. 4, p. 282 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 12, p. 281 • Wasa’il 
Al-Shi’a, Vol. 11, p. 153  
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 2, Ch. 158, H. 2911
reference.chapter: CHAPTER ON A WOMAN PERFORMING HAJJ
reference.location: Volume 2 › CHAPTER ON A WOMAN PERFORMING HAJJ
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ١٥٨، ح ٢٩١١

hadiths.chapter: 158. CHAPTER ON A WOMAN PERFORMING HAJJ
hadiths.en:
Al-Bazanti narrated from Safwan al-Jammal, who said:  
I said to Abu Abdullah {a.s}: "You know my profession. A woman comes to me, and I 
know her to be Muslim, to love you, and to hold your guardianship ( wilayah), but she 
has no mahram (close male relative)."  
Imam {a.s} said: "If a Muslim woman comes, then take her, for a believer is a mahram 
for a believing woman." He then recited the verse: 'And the believing men and believing 
women are allies of one another.' (Surah At-Tawbah 9:71).  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 2 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’a, Vol. 11, p. 153

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 2, Ch. 158, H. 2912

reference.location: Volume 2 › CHAPTER ON A WOMAN PERFORMING HAJJ
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ١٥٨، ح ٢٩١٢

hadiths.chapter: 159. CHAPTER ON A WOMAN PERFORMING HAJJ DURING
hadiths.ar:
2914  -  وَّ رَّوَّى إِن ْن ُّ ن ُّكَّي ْزٍ عَّن ْ ر ُّرَّإرَّة َّ ق َّالَّ : سَّا َّلْث ُّ إ َّن َّا عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ عَّن ِ إَّلْمَّزْإ َّة ِ إَّل َّي ِى  ي ُّي َّوَّق  َّى عَّن ْهَّا ر َّوْج ُّهَّا   إ َّ 
ي َّحُّح  ُّ ق ِى  عِذ َّي ِهَّا ق َّال
hadiths.en:
Al-'Ala' narrated from Muhammad ibn Muslim, from one of the Imams {a.s}, 
who said:  
"A divorced woman may perform Hajj during her waiting period ('iddah)."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al -Faqih, Vol. 2 • Al -Istibsar, Part 2, p. 317 • Al -Wafi, Vol. 12, p. 283 • 
Wasa’il Al-Shi’a, Vol. 11, p. 158  
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 2, Ch. 159, H. 2913
reference.chapter: CHAPTER ON A WOMAN PERFORMING HAJJ DURING
reference.location: Volume 2 › CHAPTER ON A WOMAN PERFORMING HAJJ DURING
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ١٥٩، ح ٢٩١٣

hadiths.chapter: 159. CHAPTER ON A WOMAN PERFORMING HAJJ DURING
hadiths.en:
Ibn Bukayr narrated from Zurara, who said:   
I asked Abu Abdullah {a.s} about a woman whose husband has passed away: "Can she 
perform Hajj during her waiting period ('iddah)?"  
Imam {a.s} said: "Yes."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 2 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 12, p. 284 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’a, Vol. 11, p. 159

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 2, Ch. 159, H. 2914

hadiths.chapter: 160. CHAPTER ON THE PILGRIM WHO DIES ON THE WAY
hadiths.ar:
2916   -  وَّ رَّوَّى عَّلِى  ُّ ن ْن ُّ رِن َّاب ٍ عَّن ْ ن ُّزَّي ْذٍ إَّلْعِح ْلِى ِّ ق َّالَّ : سَّا َّلْث ُّ إ َّن َّا ج َّعْق َّزٍ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ عَّن ْ رَّج ُّلٍ ج زَّح َّ جَّاج  اً وَّ مَّعَّهُّ 
ج َّمَّل  لَّهُّ وَّ ن َّق َّق َّه   وَّ ر َّإذ   ق َّمَّاب َّ ق ِى  إَّلط َّزِي ق ِ ق َّال  
وَّ إ ِن ْ كَّان َّ مَّاب َّ وَّ هُّوَّ صَّزُّورَّة   ق َّن ْلَّ إ َّن ْ ي ُّحْزِمَّ ج ُّعِلَّ ج َّمَّلُّهُّ وَّ ر َّإذُّةُّ وَّ ن َّق َّق َّي ُّهُّ وَّ مَّا مَّعَّهُّ ق ِى  جَّح  َّه ِ   إَّلْ أ ِسْلاَّمِ، ق َّا ِن ْ ق َّص َّلَّ 
مِن ْ ذ َّلِك َّ س َّى ْء  ق َّهُّوَّ لِلْوَّرَّي َّه ِ إ ِن ْ لَّمْ ي َّكُّن ْ عَّلَّي ْهِ ذَّي ْن اً ي ُّم َّ مَّاب َّ ق ِى  إَّلط َّزِي ق ِ ق َّن ْلَّ  
إ َّن ْ ي ُّحْزِمَّ لِمَّن ْ ي َّكُّون ُّ ج َّمَّلُّهُّ وَّ ن َّق َّق َّي ُّهُّ وَّ مَّا مَّعَّهُّ ق َّ ال 
ق َّي ُّق ْص َّى عَّن ْهُّ إ َّوْ ي َّكُّون َّ إ َّوْصَّى ن ِوَّصِي  َّه ٍ ق َّي َّن ْق ُّذ َّ ذ َّلِك َّ لِمَّن ْ إ َّوْصَّى لَّهُّ وَّ ي ُّح ْعَّلَّ ذ َّلِك َّ مِن ْ ي ُّلُّي ِهِ
hadiths.en:
Ali ibn Ri'ab narrated from Durays, from Abu Ja'far {a.s}, regarding a man who 
set out to perform the Hajj of Islam but died on the way.  
Imam {a.s} said: "If he died within the sacred haram, it suffices as the Hajj of Islam. But 
if he died outside the haram, his heir should perform the Hajj of Islam on his behalf."   
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 2 • Al-Kafi, Vol. 4, p. 276 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 12, p. 301 • Wasa’il 
Al-Shi’a, Vol. 11, p. 68  
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 2, Ch. 160, H. 2915
reference.chapter: CHAPTER ON THE PILGRIM WHO DIES ON THE WAY
reference.location: Volume 2 › CHAPTER ON THE PILGRIM WHO DIES ON THE WAY
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ١٦٠، ح ٢٩١٥

hadiths.chapter: 160. CHAPTER ON THE PILGRIM WHO DIES ON THE WAY
hadiths.en:
Ali ibn Ri'ab narrated from Burayd al-‘Ijli, who said:  
I asked Abu Ja'far {a.s} about a man who set out for Hajj with his camel, provisions, and 
expenses, but died on the way.  
Imam {a.s} said: "If he was a sarura (someone who had not performed the Hajj of Islam) 
and died within the haram, it suffices as the Hajj of Islam. If he died as a sarura before 
entering into Ihram, his camel, provisions, expenses, and all that he had with him 
should be used to fulfill the Hajj of Islam on his behalf. If there is anything left over, it 
goes to his heirs, provided he had no debts."   
I asked: "What if the Hajj was voluntary, and he died on the way before entering into 
Ihram? What happens to his camel, expenses, and belongings?"  
Imam {a.s} said: "Everything he had and left behind goes to his heirs unless he had debts, 
in which case they are paid from his estate. If he left a will, it is carried out for the 
person he designated, with the will being fulfilled from one -third of his estate."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 2 • Al-Kafi, Vol. 4, p. 276 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 12, p. 301 • Wasa’il 
Al-Shi’a, Vol. 11, p. 68

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 2, Ch. 160, H. 2916
reference.chapter: CHAPTER ON THE PILGRIM WHO DIES ON THE WAY
reference.location: Volume 2 › CHAPTER ON THE PILGRIM WHO DIES ON THE WAY
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ١٦٠، ح ٢٩١٦

hadiths.chapter: 161. CHAPTER ON WHAT IS PERFORMED ON BEHALF OF
hadiths.ar:
2918  -  وَّ رُّوِى َّ عَّن ْ جَّارِب ٍ ن َّي  َّاعِ إَّلْأ َّن ْمَّاطِ : إ َّن  َّهُّ سُّن ِلَّ إ َّن ُّو عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ عَّن ْ رَّج ُّلٍ إ َّوْصَّى ن ِحَّح  َّه ٍ ق َّق َّال  
كَّان َّ صَّزُّورَّة ً ق َّهِى َّ مِن ْ صُّلْث ِ مَّالِهِ إ ِن  َّمَّا هِى َّ ذَّي ْن   عَّلَّي ْهِ وَّ إ ِن ْ كَّان َّ ق َّذْ جَّح  َّ ق َّهِى َّ مِن َّ إَّلي  ُّلُّث ِ
hadiths.en:
Harun ibn Hamzah al -Ghanawi narrated from Abu Abdullah {a.s} regarding a 
man who died without performing the Hajj of Islam and left behind only enough wealth 
for the expenses of Hajj, and he had heirs.  
Imam {a.s} said: "They have the greater right to his inheritance. If they wish, they may use 
it for themselves, and if they wish, they may perform Hajj on his behalf."   
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 2 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’a, Vol. 11, p. 46  
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 2, Ch. 161, H. 2917
reference.chapter: CHAPTER ON WHAT IS PERFORMED ON BEHALF OF
reference.location: Volume 2 › CHAPTER ON WHAT IS PERFORMED ON BEHALF OF

hadiths.chapter: 161. CHAPTER ON WHAT IS PERFORMED ON BEHALF OF
hadiths.en:
It was narrated from Harith, the seller of mats, that Abu Abdullah {a.s} was 
asked about a man who had instructed in his will that a Hajj be performed on his 
behalf.  
Imam {a.s} said: "If he was a sarura (someone who had not performed the Hajj of Islam), 
it should be taken from the principal of his estate, as it is a debt upon him. But if he 
had already performed Hajj, it should be taken from one -third of his estate."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al -Faqih, Vol. 2 • Tahdhib Al -Ahkam, Vol. 9, p. 229 • Al -Wafi, Vol. 24, p. 
122 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’a, Vol. 11, p. 67

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 2, Ch. 161, H. 2918
reference.chapter: CHAPTER ON WHAT IS PERFORMED ON BEHALF OF
reference.location: Volume 2 › CHAPTER ON WHAT IS PERFORMED ON BEHALF OF

hadiths.chapter: 161. CHAPTER ON WHAT IS PERFORMED ON BEHALF OF THE DECEASED REGARDING THE PILGRIMAGE OF
hadiths.ar:
2920 - وَّ رُّوِى َّ عَّن ْ مُّعَّاوِي َّه َّ ن ْن ِ عَّم َّارٍ ق َّالَّ : سَّا َّلْث ُّ إ َّن َّا عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ عَّن ِ إِمْزَّإ َّة ٍ إ َّوْصَّث ْ ن ِمَّالٍ ق ِى  إَّ لص َّذَّق َّه ِ وَّ 
إَّلْحَّح ِّ وَّ إَّلْعِي ْق ِ ق َّق َّال ه ِ طَّان ِق َّه ً وَّ ق ِى  إَّلْعِي ْق ِ طَّان ِق َّه ً
hadiths.en:
It was narrated from Al-Harith ibn Al-Mughira, who said:  
I said to Abu Abdullah {a.s}: "My daughter instructed in her will for a Hajj to be performed, 
but she did not perform Hajj during her lifetime."  
Imam {a.s} said: "Perform Hajj on her behalf; the reward will be for both you and her."   
I said: "My mother passed away and did not perform Hajj."  
Imam {a.s} said: "Perform Hajj on her behalf; the reward will be for both you and her."   
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 2 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 12, p. 306  
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 2, Ch. 161, H. 2919

hadiths.chapter: 161. CHAPTER ON WHAT IS PERFORMED ON BEHALF OF THE DECEASED REGARDING THE PILGRIMAGE OF
hadiths.ar:
2921 - وَّ رُّوِى َّ عَّن ْ ن َّس ِي زٍ إَّلن  َّن  َّالِ ق َّالَّ : ق ُّلْث ُّ لِأ َّن ِى  عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ إ ِن  َّ وَّإلِذَّي ِى  ي ُّوُّق ِّي َّث ْ وَّ لَّمْ ي َّحُّح  َّ ق َّال 
عَّن ْهَّا رَّج ُّل  إ َّوِ إِمْزَّإ َّة  إ ِلَّى
hadiths.en:
It was narrated from Mu'awiyah ibn Ammar, who said:   
I asked Abu Abdullah {a.s} about a woman who instructed in her will for her wealth to be 
used for charity, Hajj, and freeing slaves.  
Imam {a.s} said: "Begin with Hajj, for it is obligatory. If anything remains, allocate a 
portion for charity and a portion for freeing slaves."   
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 2 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’a, Vol. 11, p. 76  
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 2, Ch. 161, H. 2920

hadiths.chapter: 161. CHAPTER ON WHAT IS PERFORMED ON BEHALF OF THE DECEASED REGARDING THE PILGRIMAGE OF
hadiths.en:
It was narrated from Basheer al-Nabbal, who said:  
I said to Abu Abdullah {a.s}: "My mother passed away and did not perform Hajj."  
Imam {a.s} said: "A man or a woman may perform Hajj on her behalf."   
I asked: "Which is more preferable to you?"  
Imam {a.s} said: "A man is more preferable to me."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 2 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 12, p. 315 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’a, Vol. 11, p. 178

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 2, Ch. 161, H. 2921

hadiths.chapter: 162. CHAPTER ON A MAN WHO MAKES A WILL FOR A PILGRIMAGE AND HIS EXECUTOR ASSIGNS IT TO SOMEONE
hadiths.en:
It was narrated from Asim ibn Humayd, from Muhammad ibn Muslim, who 
said:  
I asked Abu Ja'far {a.s} about a man who died without performing the Hajj of Islam and 
did not include it in his will. Should it be performed on his behalf?   
Imam {a.s} said: "Yes."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al -Faqih, Vol. 2 • Tahdhib Al -Ahkam, Vol. 5, p. 492 • Al -Wafi, Vol. 12, p. 
306 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’a, Vol. 11, p. 72

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 2, Ch. 162, H. 2922
